summary: Verified the savings-calculation bug fix in /api/prompt/optimize. All 36 backend pytest tests pass (27 original + 9 new tests for the bug). Frontend Copilot compare panel confirmed to show only non-negative values for both verbose and already-short prompts. Notably, a short prompt ('Reverse a string in Python.') caused the LLM to return a LONGER optimized version, and the new estimate_savings() helper correctly clamped tokens_saved / carbon_saved / cost_saved / water_saved / energy_saved / time_saved_s to exactly 0 (previously would have been negative). Math verified: for 11-token delta on Claude, carbon=0.034g (11×3.10/1000), cost=$0.00009, water=0.12ml — matches expected multipliers exactly. Empty and whitespace prompts return all zeros without crashing. estimate_tokens('') correctly returns 0. Accept flow does not decrement any total_* field.

issues: When tokens_saved is 0, the tokens tile displays '-0' (Copilot.jsx line 338 hard-codes a minus prefix: `value: `-${optimized.tokens_saved}` `). Reads awkwardly. Suggest: show plain '0' or 'No savings' when tokens_saved === 0. Also '−0 tokens' badge in header (line 312) has same issue. Not a bug — just polish.

action_items: Optional polish: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Copilot.jsx replace `-${optimized.tokens_saved}` with a conditional (show '0' or hide the minus when tokens_saved===0) to avoid the '-0 tokens' cosmetic issue in the compare panel header + tokens tile.
critical_code_review_comments: ai_service.estimate_savings correctly uses max(0, tokens_before - tokens_after) — guarantees non-negative delta before applying multipliers. Clean, minimal, correct fix.
routes.optimize() falls back to original prompt when the optimizer returns empty/whitespace — good guard.
estimate_tokens('') now returns 0 (was 1) — matches spec. Non-empty strings still return >= 1 via math.ceil(len/4).

routes.accept_optimization: coins = max(1, doc['tokens_saved'] // 5) — this still awards 1 coin even when tokens_saved is 0. That's intentional per current code (min reward for trying) but user could farm coins by accepting no-op optimizations. Not part of this bug; flagging as behavioral note.
MODEL_METRICS values used by tests are duplicated inside test file — acceptable since they're stable industry-estimate constants (documented as mock in the review request).
No changes needed elsewhere — the refactor is well contained to ai_service.estimate_savings + routes.optimize().

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the tested bug (Copilot compare panel non-negative verified for verbose + short prompt)

test_credentials: Backend tests auto-generate test_<uuid>@ecomind.app / test1234. Frontend UI test used uitest_<epoch>@ecomind.app / test1234.
seed_data_creation: None — dynamic user creation per test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix fully verified end-to-end (backend + frontend). Only minor cosmetic polish remaining is the '-0 tokens' display in Copilot.jsx when there are no savings. Full test suite is /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 36 tests, ~16s runtime. New class `TestSavingsCalculations` covers all 6 bug scenarios plus the delta-math regression. Landing page (from iteration 1 report) still needs UI verification post-r3f v9 upgrade, but that's a separate concern from this iteration.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause (fixed): Old code computed savings as `impact(tokens_before) - impact(tokens_after)`, which could go negative when the LLM returned a longer 'optimized' prompt. Fix: new `estimate_savings(before, after, model)` helper in ai_service.py first computes `delta = max(0, before - after)` and applies multipliers on the delta, guaranteeing all savings fields are >= 0. Routes now use this helper and also fall back to the original prompt when the optimizer output is empty/whitespace. Verified reproduction case: prompt='Reverse a string in Python.' (7 tokens) → LLM returns 'Write a Python function to reverse a string.' (12 tokens) → old code would have produced negative savings; new code returns exactly 0 for all savings fields.
